rationale: The citing paper uses Sarin, Challagalla, and Kohli (2012) in hypothesis development to justify a concrete mechanism: that supervisory actions can help salespeople focus on behaviors and abilities, which could raise performance under the bench program. It also reuses the cited paper's appraisal language in the discussion, recommending managers strengthen survivors' secondary appraisals after dismissals. This is more than a generic literature-list mention, even though the citing study does not directly replicate Sarin et al.'s full model or measures.
relation_description: Boichuk et al. draw on Sarin et al. as conceptual support for how supervisory actions and appraisal processes may shape salesperson responses to the bench program. The cited work informs framing and interpretation rather than being directly extended methodologically.
